title: Day 05: Gym Anxieties, Team Conrad & The Vanilla Debt Chronicles
mood: gym cowardice, TSITP relationship law, high-finance drama, peace unlocked
heroLine: Sudarshan went to Bangalore. Vanilla went to the theater. My money went to the void.
body: It's 19th June, and well guys, I didn't go to the gym today. Sudarshan went to Bangalore, and I am too afraid to go alone. So, yeah, I skipped today. Let me get some courage, or maybe my gym partner needs to stop traveling.

Series update: TSITP Season 2 completed. TBH, in Season 1, I was Team Jellyfish (Jeremiah), but by the end of Season 2, I started reminiscing about my own life. I felt Jeremiah is full-on best-friend energy—being around him just feels positive. But let's be real: girls will get bored of them very easily. Actually, those types of boys are very careless, and when they stop putting in effort at some point, then HOLA! It's a wreck. See, it even happened in "Kal Ho Naa Ho"—I mean, if SRK hadn't died, Preity Zinta would never end up with Saif. Conrad is natural, he doesn't need to put in effort to get Belly. I think that magic will stay longer than with Jer.

But anyways, a greater drama unfolded again today in my life. Actually, most of the girls I know are full-on drama queens, I guess. For our understanding, let's name her Vanilla. Vanilla is today's drama queen. Let's not go into the details—long story short, I lent her money for interest back in March 2025, and turns out she hasn't paid me back till today. Okay, not paying money is fine, but oh my god, this girl's excuses are really, really extreme. Let's dissect these, because 'we won't judge, we listen':
Excuse 1: She withdrew 50,000 and kept the money in a bag, and the bag in the college van. Turns out someone stole the bag.

Excuse 2: She started teaching Computer Science to kids. They told her they would pay her 50k for 3 months (mind you, she's from a local B.Tech college). Turns out the family fell sick, went to the hospital, and didn't pay her.
Excuse 3: She has money, but her friend's father is in the hospital so she pays for the treatment. This time, her friend's father dies.
Excuse 4: Again she has money to pay, but this time she's sick, so she spent her money on the hospital.

Excuse 5: This was a huge, huge shock to me—brace yourselves, guys. At 11:00 PM in the night, I get a view-once photo on WhatsApp: full-on blood on a letter knife beside it. I asked for my money, and she sends a suicide threat picture. (I'll attach the photo in this blog.) What would be your first thought after that?
Excuse 6: Now she has money, but a loan app automatically deducted it from her account.

Excuse 7: Now, the great part: today, 19th June 2026, with the last 12,000 left, she comes up and says her marriage is fixed with her maternal uncle (Mama). Her Mama saw all our chats and texted me with two dots ('..'). I was unknowingly chatting with him instead of her from her phone. Okay, acceptable. She says she won't pay the interest now, so she only wants to pay 5k and close it. When I ask her to pay the full amount, the drama starts again, and she wants more time.
"Enough of this drama, Vanilla. Just don't pay. Since you are so spiritual, I really hope you'll face the consequences of your own actions." And blocked.
Now, I am very, very peaceful guys, feeling very powerful now.

Anyways, my suggestion to everyone: the golden rule is, never give money to people for interest. If you are greedy for a few guaranteed return bucks, you'll always end up in a ditch. This was a very big amount for me since I am in my 3rd year of B.Tech. And since she is a girl, I don't even have the guts to ask her parents. If I ask even one sentence, my parents will ask 'why have you given that much amount?' and that's it, I am doomed.

Actually guys, why did I give it? In two lines, let me tell you. She started with 1k, 2k, 3k, then 10k. She used to pay me back—never on time, but one or two months afterwards. It's a trust built through years. She asked for money to keep a business stall in her small college (that's what she told me).

But then she went and spent 1.65 Lakhs for a biryani stall. For God's sake, her total college strength is not even 700! She only made 45k in sales, so everything was in a massive loss. And the thing is, she doesn't know anything about business—she's just a funding queen supplying cash, while her junior subordinates burn money without any knowledge.
PS: Never argue with people who lower your energy. Cut them as soon as possible, except for your love.

scraps.title: Gym Alone
scraps.text: Sudarshan went to Bangalore, so my fitness motivation stayed in bed. Skipping leg day alone.

image.alt: A quiet empty gym floor with a rack of iron dumbbells in the foreground, photographic style

scraps.title: The Money Feud
scraps.text: A year of trust built on small loans, destroyed by extreme excuses and locked behind a block button.

image.alt: A phone displaying messages about money on a dark table, with blurred silhouettes of a man and woman in the background

scraps.title: The 11:00 PM Repayment Request Response
scraps.text: Asked for repayment, got a view-once photo of blood on a letter knife.

image.alt: WhatsApp screenshot showing a letter knife on top of a letter with fake blood, padded to fit 4:3 frame

scraps.title: The Golden Rule
scraps.text: Never give money to people for interest. If you are greedy for a few guaranteed return bucks, you'll always end up in a ditch.

The 11:00 PM shocker. Debt collection turned emergency services.
